settings.title: Settings
settings.daynightLinearChange: Linear transition between day and night
settings.minimalDehumidifierOffTimeHint: Minimum time in seconds the fridge/dehumidifier stays off before it can turn on again. Prevents short-cycling of the compressor.
settings.minimalDehumidifierOffTimeWarning: Warning: Values below 240s may damage the fridge compressor due to short-cycling!
settings.heaterHysteresisHint: The cabinet heater turns on once the temperature drops this far below the target, and off again once the target is reached. Smaller values hold the temperature tighter but make the heater cycle more often.
settings.heaterDwellHint: Minimum time the heater stays on resp. off before it may switch again. Prevents the smart socket from being switched constantly. Values below 30s have no effect, as the smart socket is only commanded every 30s.
settings.heaterDehumidifyLimitHint: How far the temperature may drop below the target while the fridge is running to dehumidify. At the limit, dehumidifying is suspended and the heater may counteract.
settings.heaterDehumidifyAssist: Allow the heater to counteract while dehumidifying
settings.heaterAssistLeadHint: How far above the fridge temperature limit the heater already kicks in while dehumidifying. The heater's warmth arrives with a delay — without a lead it only engages once the temperature has already fallen through the limit. Larger values hold the temperature steadier but let the heater and fridge work against each other for longer. 0 means no lead, in which case the heater practically never counteracts while dehumidifying.
settings.useLongHumidityAvg: Use long humidity average (240 instead of 100 values)

alarms.title: Alarms
alarms.upperThresholdHint: Alarm triggers when value goes above this
alarms.lowerThresholdHint: Alarm triggers when value goes below this
alarms.thresholdSecondsHint: Number of seconds the threshold must be exceeded before the alarm is triggered
alarms.actionTargetHint: Email address or webhook URL - if you want to use different targets for triggered and resolved alarms, separate them with a pipe '|' character. E.g. "http://example.com/turn-on|http://example.com/turn-off"
alarms.cooldownSecondsHint: How many seconds until the same alarm can be triggered again. For E-Mail actions, the minimum is 300.
alarms.retriggerSecondsHint: Seconds until the current alarm state is retriggered. In case an alarm state is not transmitted successfully, this ensures that the alarm state will be sent again after this time. (Minimum: 60)
alarms.name: Alarm Name
alarms.webhookMethodHint: Choose HTTP method for webhook requests.
alarms.webhookHeadersHint: Add custom HTTP headers to be sent with the webhook request.
alarms.webhookTriggeredPayloadHint: Custom payload sent when the alarm is triggered (for POST/PUT). Leave empty to send the default payload.
alarms.webhookResolvedPayloadHint: Custom payload sent when the alarm is resolved (for POST/PUT). Leave empty to send the default payload.
alarms.tunnelWebhookHint: If enabled, webhook requests will be tunneled through the module. This is helpful if your webhook URL is not directly reachable from the internet. WARNING: HTTPS may not work properly when tunneling is enabled.

settings.ppfdLuxFactorHint: PPFD is estimated as lux × this factor. It depends on the light spectrum (white LED ≈ 0.015, HPS ≈ 0.0122, sunlight ≈ 0.0185). Calibrate it to your grow light. Default 0.015.
settings.rtspStreamHint: Enter the URL of your webcam here to enable webcam streaming in the portal, e.g. "rtsp://user:password@my-public-hostname-or-ip.com:554/". The input here must be processable by FFMPEG.
settings.maintenanceWebcamOff: Webcam OFF on maintenance mode or mode OFF
settings.maintenanceWebcamOffHint: If enabled, the webcam will automatically stop capturing images when the device is in maintenance mode or the device's mode is set to OFF.
settings.tunnelRtspStreamHint: If enabled, the webcam stream will be tunneled through the module. This is helpful if your webcam is not directly reachable from the internet.
settings.deleteDeviceConfirmText: This removes the device from your account — it disappears from the app until it's claimed again.

share.title: Share this page

intro.charts: Create a link that lets anyone view this chart page - no account needed.
intro.diary: Create a link that lets anyone view this diary - no account needed.

share.viewOnlyHint: Visitors see exactly this view and cannot change it.

interactiveHint.charts: Visitors can change the time frame, measures, and log filter.
interactiveHint.diary: Visitors can switch reports, change strain and filters, and view the webcam.

share.includeChartsHint: Visitors can open the chart views linked from the grow report.
share.manageHint: Under “Shared links” in the menu you can see how often the link was opened and revoke it at any time.
share.noLinks: No shared links yet. Use the share button on a chart or diary page to create one.
share.revokeConfirmText: The link stops working immediately. This cannot be undone.
share.expiredTitle: This link is no longer valid
share.expiredText: The share link has expired or was revoked by its owner.

login.welcomeSubtitle: Monitor and control your grow — from anywhere.
login.registerSubtitle: One portal for all your Terp Control devices.

legacy.toggle: Moving over from the old Fridge Grow 2.0 / Plantalytix cloud?
legacy.intro: Connect your existing module to the Terp Control cloud (instructions in German):

messages.notActivated: You need to activate your account first. Please check your inbox.
messages.accountCreatedText: An activation code has been sent to your email address.
messages.accountActivatedText: Your account has been activated, you can now log in:
messages.emailInUse: This email address is already in use.
messages.wrongRecoveryToken: This recovery link is invalid or has expired.
messages.passwordChangedText: Your password has been changed, you can now log in:

photoperiod.name: Photoperiod
photoperiod.description: Classic seeds, approx. 16 weeks. Flowering starts with the 12/12 light switch.

autoflower.name: Autoflower
autoflower.description: Automatic flowering, approx. 11 weeks. Light stays at 18 hours.

confirmations.startFlowering: Ready for flowering? From now on the light runs 12/12 — confirm to start the switch.
confirmations.harvest: Check the trichomes: milky with first amber tones? Then harvest and confirm — drying starts next.
confirmations.dryingDone: Do the branches snap instead of bending? Then drying is done — off to the curing jars.

assistant.confirmationPending: Your plan is waiting for a confirmation — open settings
assistant.deviationMonitorHint: Your device only monitors — adjust this manually in your setup.
assistant.bannerText: Let a grow plan guide you through the stages — targets adjust automatically, and you confirm the big steps.

seedling.1: Keep the medium slightly moist, never soaking wet — seedlings drown faster than they dry out.
seedling.2: High humidity (65–75 %) helps young roots; a propagator dome works great in the first days.
seedling.3: Keep the light dimmed (around 40 %) and at a good distance — bleached or curled leaves mean it's too strong.
seedling.4: Don't feed yet: the seed carries nutrients for the first two weeks.

vegetative.1: Water when the top few centimeters of the medium feel dry — lifting the pot tells you more than a schedule.
vegetative.2: Now is training time: topping or bending (LST) creates an even canopy and more bud sites.
vegetative.3: Raise light intensity step by step towards 80–100 % as the plants toughen up.
vegetative.4: Watch the VPD chart: 0.8–1.1 kPa keeps transpiration and growth in balance.

flowering.1: Keep humidity at or below 50 % — dense buds plus high humidity is how mold starts.
flowering.2: Don't change the light schedule anymore; interruptions of the dark period stress photoperiod plants.
flowering.3: Feed bloom nutrients and watch leaf color — fading towards the end is normal.
flowering.4: Check the trichomes in the last weeks: cloudy with the first amber ones usually means harvest time.

drying.1: 16–20 °C and 55–60 % humidity, dark and with gentle air movement — slow drying preserves the terpenes.
drying.2: Plan for 10–14 days. If it's done after 5, it was too warm or too dry.
drying.3: The snap test: when small branches crack instead of bending, the buds are ready for curing jars.
drying.4: Open the jars daily in the first curing week ('burping') to let moisture escape.

chartPresets.saveTitle: Save this view as a preset

wizard.title: Setup assistant
wizard.errorSaving: Saving failed. Check your connection and try again.

name.title: Name your device
name.text: Pick a name you'll recognize — for example "Tent living room" or "Fridge basement".
name.label: Device name

connections.title: What does your controller switch?
connections.text: Your controller switches through paired smart sockets — the app detects automatically what is connected. Pairing happens right on the controller (Settings → Smart Sockets).
connections.noSockets: No sockets paired yet — monitoring only. You can pair some on the controller anytime; the app adapts automatically.
connections.unknown: Your controller reports its socket status only after a firmware update. Until then all control options are shown.
connections.detected-full: Detected: your controller regulates the climate.
connections.detected-light_only: Detected: your controller only switches the light — climate values are monitored and targets act as reference values.
connections.detected-monitor: Detected: monitoring only — nothing is switched, targets act as reference values.

stage.title: Where is your grow right now?
stage.text: We fill in proven target values for this stage — you can fine-tune everything later.

plan.title: Just targets, or a full grow plan?
plan.text: The grow plan moves through the stages for you, adjusts the targets automatically and asks before big changes.
plan.textMonitor: In monitoring mode the plan tracks your stages, keeps the diary up to date and shows you the recommended ranges per stage.
plan.targetsText: Set values for the chosen stage once.
plan.confirmNote: Before the flowering switch, harvest and the end of drying, the plan waits for your confirmation — you stay in charge.
plan.startsAtStage: Phases you're already past keep their defaults — you only plan from your current phase onward.

done.title: That's it — the automation takes over
done.titleMonitor: That's it — your monitoring is live
done.text: Your device now regulates towards the target values around the clock. If everything runs well, you don't have to do a thing.
done.textMonitor: You now see live values, history charts and get alarms when something drifts off. Your targets serve as reference ranges.
done.auxHint: You can connect your webcam and Smart Sockets anytime in the device settings under "Connected devices".

onboarding.welcomeText: Connect your first Terp Control device and let the automation take over — you keep an eye on everything from anywhere.
onboarding.step1Text: Power it up and connect it to your WiFi — the display guides you through it.
onboarding.step2Title: Read the code off the display
onboarding.step2Text: Your device shows a short pairing code under "Connect to portal".
onboarding.step3Text: The device appears in your portal and the setup assistant takes over.
onboarding.claimFailed: That code didn't work. Check the code on the device display and try again.
onboarding.helpText: Need help getting connected? All guides are on

charts.title: Charts
charts.logsNoEntries: No log messages for the selected timespan
charts.logsNoFilteredEntries: No log messages for the selected filters
charts.moreHiddenMessages: There are {{count}} more messages. Please reduce your selection.

fridge.title: Terp Control FRIDGE GROW

settings.floating-day: Use custom duration for day/night cycle

fridge.manualModeOfflineWarning: If your device is offline, changes made in settings mode will not be applied until the device comes back online.

recipe.warning: Warning: Recipes rely on a steady internet connection. If the connection is lost, the recipe may not advance to the next step as expected.

co2inject.name: Automatic speed reduction
co2inject.description: This fan is paired to a Plantaltix smart socket in CO2 injection mode and its speed will automatically be reduced during CO2 injection

plug.title: Terp Control SMART SOCKET
plug.co2sensor-text: This mode requires an optional CO2 sensor to work.

settings.heater-on-desc: Turns output ON when the measured temperature falls BELOW this value
settings.heater-off-desc: Turns output OFF when the measured temperature rises ABOVE this value
settings.cooler-on-desc: Turns output ON when the measured temperature rises ABOVE this value
settings.cooler-off-desc: Turns output OFF when the measured temperature falls BELOW this value
settings.humidify-on-desc: Turns output ON when the measured humidity falls BELOW this value
settings.humidify-off-desc: Turns output OFF when the measured humidity rises ABOVE this value
settings.dehumidify-on-desc: Turns output ON when the measured humidity rises ABOVE this value
settings.dehumidify-off-desc: Turns output OFF when the measured humidity falls BELOW this value

overtemperature.limit-desc: Socket will be disabled if measured temperature rises above this limit
overtemperature.hysteresis-desc: Temperature must fall this value below limit for socket to be reenabled

undertemperature.limit-desc: Socket will be disabled if measured temperature falls below this limit
undertemperature.hysteresis-desc: Temperature must rise this value above limit for socket to be reenabled

time.min_on-desc: Socket will not turn off for a minimum of this time after being turned on
time.min_off-desc: Socket will not turn on for a minimum of this time after being turned off

diary.title: Diary
diary.gapPhotos: View photos from the days in between
diary.webcamTime: Time of day of the photo

message-lost-connection-text: The connection between your device and our sever was lost and has been reestablished. If this messages appeares regulary please check your wifi and internet connection
message-device-booted-text: Your device has rebooted. If this messages appears regularly please check your power connection.
message-device-booted:POWERON-text: Your device has rebooted. The reason was a power cycle (POWERON) - this is normal after unplugging or a power outage. If this message appears unexpectedly please check your power connection.
message-device-booted:BROWNOUT-text: Your device has rebooted. The reason was a brownout: the supply voltage dropped below the safe threshold. This usually means the power adapter or USB cable cannot deliver enough current.
message-device-booted:TASK_WDT-text: Your device has rebooted. The reason was a task watchdog timeout: the main firmware loop did not respond in time and was force-restarted. This typically happens when network operations get stuck on a flaky internet uplink. If this message appears regularly please check your internet connection.
message-device-booted:INT_WDT-text: Your device has rebooted. The reason was an interrupt watchdog timeout: an interrupt routine in the firmware took too long. Please report this to support if it happens repeatedly.
message-device-booted:WDT-text: Your device has rebooted. The reason was an internal watchdog timeout. Please report this to support if it happens repeatedly.
message-device-booted:PANIC-text: Your device has rebooted. The reason was a firmware crash (panic) - the device restarted automatically. Please report this to support if it happens repeatedly.
message-device-booted:SW-text: Your device has rebooted. The reason was an intentional software-triggered restart (for example after a configuration change or firmware update).
message-device-booted:EXT-text: Your device has rebooted. The reason was a hardware reset via the reset pin.
message-device-booted:DEEPSLEEP-text: Your device has rebooted. The reason was a wake-up from deep sleep mode.
message-device-booted:SDIO-text: Your device has rebooted. The reason was a restart over the SDIO interface.
message-device-booted:UNKNOWN-text: Your device has rebooted. The reason was not reported by the device. If this message appears regularly please check your power connection and internet uplink.
message-device-booted:REMOTE-text: Your device has rebooted. The reason was an intentional restart triggered remotely via the portal.
message-buffer-overflow-text: Data could not be transfered to the server and was lost. Please check your internet connection or wifi.
message-device-firmware-update-text: The software of your device was updated
message-ext-sensor-deviate-text: External and internal sensor differ! Check sensor placement!
message-maintenance-mode-activated:0-tex: Your device's maintenance mode was manually disabled. Alarms will be re-enabled in 10 minutes.
message-maintenance-mode-activated-text: Your device was manually put into maintenance mode for {{value}} minutes. Alarms will be disabled during this time
message-maintenance-mode-activated-remot: Your device's maintenance mode was manually disabled via the portal. Alarms will be re-enabled in 10 minutes.
Your device was manually put into maintenance mode for {{value}} minutes via the portal. Alarms will be disabled during this time
message-firmware-update-complete-text: You device's firmware update was completed
message-firmware-update-complete-with-id: Your device's firmware update was completed: {{value}}
message-recipe-advanced-text: The recipe has advanced to step {{value}}.
message-recipe-looped-text: The recipe has looped back to step #1 ({{value}}).
message-recipe-completed-text: The recipe has completed all steps.
message-recipe-step-manually-activated-t: Recipe step {{value}} has been manually activated by the user.
message-rtsp-stream-error-text: Failed to fetch image from RTSP stream: {{value}}
message-smart-socket-cmd-failed-text: Failed to send a power command to the smart socket for role: {{value}}
message-smart-socket-connected-text: A smart socket was connected for role: {{value}}
message-smart-socket-disconnected-text: The smart socket assignment was removed for role: {{value}}

simpleSettings.referenceBannerMonitor: This controller only monitors. Your targets act as reference ranges for charts, the assistant and alarms — nothing is switched.
simpleSettings.referenceBannerLightOnly: This controller only drives the lamp. Climate targets act as reference ranges — heating or humidity are not switched.
simpleSettings.stageText: Pick the stage your plants are in — we set proven targets. Fine-tune anything below.
simpleSettings.stageTextReference: Pick the stage your plants are in — we set proven reference values. Fine-tune anything below.
simpleSettings.deviceOff: The device is switched off. Pick a grow stage above to start regulating.
simpleSettings.expertHint: Fine-tuning like heater behavior, dehumidify timing or alarms lives in expert mode (switch at the top).

plan.title: Grow plan
plan.editHint: The values below apply to the current stage. Saving keeps the plan running.
plan.upcomingHint: Tap a duration to adjust the remaining plan. Changes apply when you save.

light.title: Light & day rhythm
light.hint: Defines when your lamp is on and how bright it runs. The day targets below apply while the light is on.
light.hintMonitor: Defines which hours count as day. The day targets below are evaluated during these hours.
light.scheduleText: Seedlings and vegetative plants usually get 18 h, flowering runs at 12 h. Autoflowers stay at 18 h all the way.
light.intensityHint: Start seedlings dimmed (about 40%) and increase towards 100% in flowering.

co2.title: CO2
co2.hint: Only useful with a CO2 bottle and valve. Without one, keep 400 ppm (ambient level) — the valve then never opens.
co2.text: Without CO2 enrichment leave this at 400 ppm. With a CO2 setup, 900–1200 ppm speeds up growth.

notifications.title: Notifications & alarms
notifications.empty: No alarms yet. Add one from a template — e.g. heat, humidity or an empty CO2 bottle.
notifications.pickPreset: What do you want to watch?
notifications.pickChannel: how do you want to be notified?
notifications.savedNote: Alarms are stored when you save the settings.

webcam.title: Webcam
webcam.intro: Watch your grow live and in the diary timelapse. Pick your camera:

terp_cam.name: Terp Control Cam
terp_cam.subtitle: Our camera — connects via your controller

webcam.terpCamFound: Terp Control Cam detected — your controller reported its stream URL.
webcam.terpCamActive: Terp Control Cam connected. The stream is tunneled through your controller.
webcam.terpCamMissing: No Terp Control Cam reported yet. Connect it on your controller first: Settings → Terp Cam → connect cam.
webcam.hostHintTunnel: Local IP of the camera in your home network — the stream is fetched through your device's tunnel.
webcam.hostHintPublic: Public IP or DDNS name reachable from the internet. Enable tunneling to use a local IP instead.
webcam.savedNote: Webcam changes are stored when you save the settings.
webcam.pickModel: Which camera do you have? (one webcam per device)
webcam.placeholderHint: Replace <user>, <password> and <ip> in the URL with your camera's values.
webcam.customHint: Format: rtsp://user:password@ip-or-hostname:554/path — you can find the stream path in your camera’s manual. HTTP(S) snapshot or MJPEG URLs work too.
webcam.customHintTunnel: If the camera sits on the same network as your device, enable “Tunnel webcam stream” and use its local IP.

sockets.title: Smart Sockets
sockets.intro: Your controller switches these sockets. Pairing happens directly on the controller (Settings → Smart Sockets).
sockets.unknownFirmware: This firmware doesn't report its sockets yet. Update the device firmware or manage sockets on the device.
sockets.removeConfirmText: The "{{role}}" socket will be unpaired and reset. This takes effect immediately.
sockets.offlineHint: The device is offline — socket changes need a connected device.
sockets.pairOnDevice: Removal takes effect immediately. To pair a new socket use the controller's display.
sockets.editHint: The controller switches the socket at this address (Tasmota HTTP). Leave the password empty to keep the pairing credentials.
sockets.testNote: Test switches the socket on for about 2 seconds.

terp.name: Terp Control Smart Socket
terp.subtitle: Our socket — pairs via the controller
terp.hint: Pair it directly on your controller: Settings → Smart Sockets → connect socket. It appears here automatically afterwards.

sockets.reachabilityHint: Switching always happens locally from the controller — the socket only needs to be reachable by it. No internet exposure is required.

humidity_high.name: Humidity too high
humidity_high.description: Mold risk — threshold follows the stage (60% in flower, 75% otherwise)

co2_empty.name: CO2 bottle empty
co2_empty.description: CO2 stays below 350 ppm despite enrichment

running_continuously.name: Dehumidifier running non-stop
running_continuously.description: Output on for more than 30 minutes straight

discord.name: Discord
discord.hint: Discord → channel settings → Integrations → Webhooks → New webhook, then paste the URL here.

telegram.name: Telegram
telegram.hint: Create a bot with @BotFather, add it to your chat and get the chat ID e.g. via @userinfobot.

ntfy.name: ntfy.sh
ntfy.hint: Install the ntfy app and subscribe to the same topic — no account needed. Pick a hard-to-guess topic name.

home_assistant.name: Home Assistant
home_assistant.hint: Create a webhook trigger in an automation; local instances are reached through your device's tunnel automatically.

custom.name: Custom
custom.hint: Configure URL, method, headers and payload yourself below.
